legionTrait.name: Paragons of Battle
legionTrait.description: Dark Angels may take 1 free Champion upgrade per 750 points and may upgrade any unit which has access to the Consul upgrade. In addition, units with a Champion, Lieutenant Commander, or Supreme Commander upgrade may add +1 to their Close Combat value (up to a maximum of 2+).

legionTrait.name: Exemplars of War
legionTrait.description: Once per game, a player may elect to add 1 to their Strategy Rating when rolling for Initiative. For that turn, they may also re-roll any one additional failed Action test (you may not re-roll a re-roll). In addition, for that turn, any Character unit may add +1 to their Close Combat value (up to a maximum of 2+).

legionTrait.name: Brick by Brick
legionTrait.description: While taking a Shooting action, Iron Warriors impart a -1 modifier to enemy cover saves. Furthermore, Iron Warrior Infantry Character units gain the Siege trait.

legionTrait.name: Wind Born
legionTrait.description: Legion Light Vehicles and Armoured Vehicles ignore the -1 penalty to shooting attacks when taking a Double action. In addition, all Aircraft, Light Vehicles, Armoured Vehicles, and units with the Mounted trait may re-roll all failed dangerous terrain checks.

legionTrait.name: Loping Advance
legionTrait.description: Space Wolf Character units gain the Brutal trait. In addition, Infantry units in this Legion gain +5cm to their countercharge and consolidation moves.

legionTrait.name: Disciplined Doctrine
legionTrait.description: Imperial Fist Tactical, Breacher, Phalanx Warder and Templar Brethren units may re-roll failed To Hit rolls of 1 when firing in the Firefight phase, and any formation containing at least one Phalanx Warder or Templar Brethren unit counts as having a Champion for the purposes of formation-level rules.

legionTrait.name: Unspeakable Horrors
legionTrait.description: When claiming a Crossfire bonus against an opponent, the first two casualties inflict an additional Blast marker against an opponent instead of just one. Enemy formations attempting to Rally within 30cm of a Night Lords formation suffer an additional -1 penalty to their Rally test.

legionTrait.name: The Red Thirst
legionTrait.description: Blood Angels Assault and Inductii units gain First strike in the first turn of any assault they initiate. In addition, Angel's Tears, Crimson Paladin and Dawnbreaker units automatically pass any Leadership test required to charge an enemy formation.

legionTrait.name: Iron Resolve
legionTrait.description: When rolling to save, Iron Hands units ignore the effects of weapons with the Armourbane trait. Furthermore, 1 formation that consists of at least half Armoured Vehicles may set up as a Garrison unit. This Legion may not take Librarians.

factions.name: World Eaters
factions.subtitle: XII Legion · The Eaters of Worlds

legionTrait.name: Bloodlust
legionTrait.description: When taking an Engage action, Infantry and units with the Walker trait add +1 to their Close Combat value (up to a maximum of 2+).

factions.name: Ultramarines
factions.subtitle: XIII Legion · The Children of Guilliman

legionTrait.name: Interlocking Tactics
legionTrait.description: When issuing an order to a unit, you may also attempt a second identical order to an unbroken unit within 10cm with the same roll, taking -1 if either unit has accumulated Blast markers. You may then activate them in either order (if the first unit fails its Action test it must Hold; the second may still attempt to activate later). Formations may also add +1 to any Rally test if there are friendly units within 30cm of any unit in that formation.

legionTrait.name: Indefatigable
legionTrait.description: Death Guard formations consisting of at least half Infantry units may automatically pass Action tests when taking an Advance action. In addition, their Infantry Characters are so tough they gain the Reinforced armour trait if they do not already have it.

legionTrait.name: Might and Magic
legionTrait.description: Thousand Sons may take 1 free Librarian upgrade per 750 points, and may upgrade any unit which has access to the Consul upgrade. In addition, units with Consul upgrades may add +1 to any Invulnerable save.

legionTrait.name: Death Dealers
legionTrait.description: When taking an Engage action, Infantry units add +1 to their Firefight value (up to a maximum of 2+).

factions.name: Word Bearers
factions.subtitle: XVII Legion · The Bearers of the Word

legionTrait.name: Fanatical Zeal
legionTrait.description: Word Bearers armies may take 1 free Chaplain upgrade per 1,500 points. Additionally, when making a shooting attack or taking an Engage action against a Broken formation, add 1 additional Blast marker to the final result if any wounds were caused. You may also remove 1 additional Blast marker from any formation when making a successful Rally test.

legionTrait.name: Promethean Cult
legionTrait.description: Salamanders units never lose a point of Firefight or Close Combat from suppression. In addition, any Salamander formation containing at least one Firedrake Terminator or Pyroclast unit may re-roll a single failed save per turn.

legionTrait.name: Strike From the Shadows
legionTrait.description: All Raven Guard infantry formations may add +5cm to their speed on the first turn of the game. In addition, any Raven Guard formation made up entirely of units with the Scout or Infiltrate ability may be deployed using the Infiltrate special rule regardless of its formation type.

legionTrait.name: Twin Serpents
legionTrait.description: All Character units gain the First Strike trait. Once per turn, if a Character unit is removed as a casualty and there is a friendly, unengaged, unbroken formation within 15cm, you may replace one non-character unit with a character of the same type as the one removed, so long as it may take the Consul upgrade. (Note: this may lead to formations having multiple character units.)

factions.name: Solar Auxilia
factions.subtitle: Excertus Imperialis · Cohorts of the Hundred Worlds

legionTrait.name: Cohorts of the Solar Auxilia
legionTrait.description: Strategy Rating 3 · Initiative 2+. May field 2 Support per Line, with up to 3 Upgrades per formation. One free Legate Commander per full 750 points (does not cost points).

legionTrait.name: Provenances of the Imperial Army
legionTrait.description: Strategy Rating 2 · Initiative 2+. May field 2 Support per Line, with up to 3 Upgrades per formation. Select up to two Provenances; each formation chooses one to modify INF stats (Determined, Feral Warriors, Survivors, Traitors, Void Fighters, Warrior Elite).

legionTrait.name: Noble Lances
legionTrait.description: Strategy Rating 3 · Initiative 2+. Formations gain +1 to Engage Action tests and +1 to Rally tests. 2 Support per Line, max 3 Upgrades per formation. Optional Household Trait: Second Greatest Predator — +1 to hit in assault vs AV character units; force enemy critical-hit rerolls.

legionTrait.name: Engines of the Omnissiah
legionTrait.description: Strategy Rating 3 · Initiative 1+. 2 Support per Line; max 3 Upgrades per formation. Special rules: Void Shields. Optional Legio Trait: Heart of Steel (reroll critical-hit damage results) OR Warhorn (15cm AOE Blast markers on a 5+).

factions.name: Mechanicum Taghmata
factions.subtitle: Cult of Mars · Phalanx of the Omnissiah

legionTrait.name: Servants of the Omnissiah
legionTrait.description: Strategy Rating 3 · Initiative 2+. 3 Support per Line, max 4 Upgrades per formation, 1/3 cap on Lords of War/Allies. Special Rules: Automaton, Cortex Controller / Cybernetica Cortex. Optional Mechanicum Trait: Targeting Algorithm — Tech-Priest or Magos formations may forgo countercharge to add +1 Firefight in assault.

factions.name: Legio Custodes
factions.subtitle: The Emperor's Chosen · Companions of the Golden Throne

legionTrait.name: The Emperor's Chosen
legionTrait.description: Strategy Rating 6 · Initiative 1+. 3 Support per Line, max 4 Upgrades per formation, up to 1/2 of total points on Lords of War/Allies. Custodes units take 2 Blast markers to suppress; formations only break at 2 BM per unit; ignore -1 Rally if enemies within 30cm; halve hackdowns from lost assaults.

factions.name: Daemons of the Ruinstorm
factions.subtitle: Spawn of the Warp · The Four Powers Unleashed

legionTrait.name: Echoes of the Ruinstorm
legionTrait.description: Strategy Rating 2 · Initiative 2+. 1 Support per Line, 0-1 Lord of War per Line, any number of Upgrades per formation (each only once). Special Rules: Instability (failed Action test costs 1D3 Lesser Daemons), Chaos Gate, Daemonic Assault.

dark-angels.name: Dark Angels
dark-angels.subtitle: I Legion · The First
dark-angels.lore: The First Legion, raised on the death-world of Caliban under the watchful eye of the Lion. Secretive, knightly and ancient, the Dark Angels keep the Emperor's faith with cold steel, hidden orders and grim resolve. Where lesser Legions break ranks, the Sons of Caliban form their unbroken line.

legionTrait.name: Paragons of Battle
legionTrait.description: Dark Angels may take 1 free Champion upgrade per 750 points and may upgrade any unit which has access to the Consul upgrade. In addition, units with a Champion, Lieutenant Commander, or Supreme Commander upgrade may add +1 to their Close Combat value (up to a maximum of 2+).

emperors-children.name: Emperor's Children
emperors-children.subtitle: III Legion · The Phoenician's Own
emperors-children.lore: Fulgrim's chosen, the III Legion are the Emperor's masterwork of perfection. Their every blow is a study in lethal grace, their parade-ground precision matched only by their pitiless cruelty in the wake of the Phoenician's fall.

legionTrait.description: Once per game, a player may elect to add 1 to their Strategy Rating when rolling for Initiative. For that turn, they may also re-roll any one additional failed Action test (you may not re-roll a re-roll). In addition, for that turn, any Character unit may add +1 to their Close Combat value (up to a maximum of 2+).

iron-warriors.subtitle: IV Legion · The Olympians
iron-warriors.lore: Perturabo's grim engineers excel at the brutal arithmetic of siegecraft. Where they march, walls fall and warriors are broken upon the anvil of their guns. They take no joy in war — only the cold satisfaction of a problem solved.

legionTrait.name: Brick by Brick

white-scars.name: White Scars
white-scars.subtitle: V Legion · The Storm Riders
white-scars.lore: Hailing from the steppes of Chogoris, the V Legion ride to war with the speed of the open sky. Jaghatai's sons strike like a hurricane and are gone before retribution can land, the thunder of their guns the only mark left in their wake.

legionTrait.name: Wind Born
legionTrait.description: Legion Light Vehicles and Armoured Vehicles ignore the -1 penalty to shooting attacks when taking a Double action. In addition, all Aircraft, Light Vehicles, Armoured Vehicles, and units with the Mounted trait may re-roll all failed dangerous terrain checks.

imperial-fists.name: Imperial Fists
imperial-fists.subtitle: VII Legion · The Emperor's Praetorians
imperial-fists.lore: The VII Legion, sons of Rogal Dorn and chosen guardians of the Imperial Palace. Stoic, methodical and possessed of unmatched discipline, the Imperial Fists are the masters of siege warfare — equally peerless in breaching a fortress and in holding one against impossible odds. Where the line must hold, the Fists are there.

legionTrait.name: Disciplined Doctrine
legionTrait.description: Imperial Fist Tactical, Breacher, Phalanx Warder and Templar Brethren units may re-roll failed To Hit rolls of 1 when firing in the Firefight phase, and any formation containing at least one Phalanx Warder or Templar Brethren unit counts as having a Champion for the purposes of formation-level rules.

night-lords.name: Night Lords
night-lords.subtitle: VIII Legion · The Murder Sky
night-lords.lore: In the dark and twisted tapestry of the Horus Heresy, the Night Lords Legion prowl the stars as a chilling spectre of terror and brutality. Reborn on shadow-shrouded Nostramo and led by their enigmatic Primarch Konrad Curze, they are masters of terror tactics and psychological warfare. They are the princes of the night — dominance and terror resplendent in midnight clad.

legionTrait.name: Unspeakable Horrors
legionTrait.description: When claiming a Crossfire bonus against an opponent, the first two casualties inflict an additional Blast marker against an opponent instead of just one. Enemy formations attempting to Rally within 30cm of a Night Lords formation suffer an additional -1 penalty to their Rally test.

compositionRules: Max 3 Support detachments per Line detachment in the army.
Max 4 Upgrades per formation.
Max 1/3 of total points value may be spent on Lords of War or Allies.
Primarch Konrad Curze may only be taken once and counts as your Supreme Commander.
Destroyer Detachment: 1 per 1,000 pts.
Night Raptor Detachment: 1 per 1,000 pts.
Terror Troop Detachment: 1 per 2,000 pts.

blood-angels.name: Blood Angels
blood-angels.subtitle: IX Legion · Angels of Baal
blood-angels.lore: The IX Legion, sons of Sanguinius and bearers of the genetic legacy of their angelic Primarch. Beautiful, terrible and tireless in war, the Blood Angels fight with the grace of host of heaven and the fury of a sun rising over the battlefield. Where they descend, the enemy is undone in a single glorious charge.

legionTrait.name: The Red Thirst
legionTrait.description: Blood Angels Assault and Inductii units gain First strike in the first turn of any assault they initiate. In addition, Angel's Tears, Crimson Paladin and Dawnbreaker units automatically pass any Leadership test required to charge an enemy formation.

compositionRules: Max 3 Support detachments per Line detachment in the army.
Max 4 Upgrades per formation.
Max 1/3 of total points value may be spent on Lords of War or Allies.
Angel's Tears Detachment and Crimson Paladin Detachment: 1 per 2,000 pts each. (Crimson Paladin cap is TBD in source.)
Dawnbreaker Cohort: 1 per 1,000 pts.
Primarch Sanguinius may only be taken once and counts as your Supreme Commander.

iron-hands.name: Iron Hands
iron-hands.subtitle: X Legion · The Gorgon's Sons
iron-hands.lore: From the crucible of the Great Crusade, the Iron Hands Legion and their taciturn Primarch Ferrus Manus carved out a reputation as relentless, dogged warriors. Forged on Medusa, they believe that weakness is unacceptable and seek to harden themselves through the cold application of logic, cybernetic augmentation and overwhelming firepower. Clad in rugged black armour, they advance in unrelenting tides — but beneath their cold exterior lies a deep-seated mistrust of weakness and a burning desire to purge it.

legionTrait.name: Iron Resolve
legionTrait.description: When rolling to save, Iron Hands units ignore the effects of weapons with the Armourbane trait. Furthermore, 1 formation that consists of at least half Armoured Vehicles may set up as a Garrison unit. This Legion may not take Librarians.

Max 4 Upgrades per formation.
Max 1/3 of total points value may be spent on Lords of War or Allies.
Primarch Ferrus Manus may only be taken once and counts as your Supreme Commander.
Medusan Immortal Detachment: 1 per 1,000 pts.
Iron Hands may not take Librarian upgrades.

world-eaters.name: World Eaters
world-eaters.subtitle: XII Legion · The Eaters of Worlds
world-eaters.lore: In the savage chronicles of the Great Crusade, the World Eaters were a byword for carnage and destruction. Once they left their old titles and names behind on the world of Nuceria, they emerged as the paragons of savagery, brutality and unbridled rage, their ranks filled with warriors whose archeotech Butcher's Nails feed an endless thirst for slaughter. With Primarch Angron at their head, they march to war consumed by the thirst for battle, ready to sacrifice everything in the name of glory and vengeance.

ultramarines.name: Ultramarines
ultramarines.subtitle: XIII Legion · The Children of Guilliman
ultramarines.lore: In the sprawling morass of the Horus Heresy, the Ultramarines Legion represents a shining beacon of hope amidst the darkness. From their embattled fortresses in the Realm of Ultramar in the galactic east, they strive to embody the virtues of duty, honour, and unyielding resolve. Headed by their legendary Primarch Roboute Guilliman, they are the paragons of martial virtue and guardians of humanity, their weapons honed to a razor edge and their formations textbook-perfect.

legionTrait.name: Interlocking Tactics
legionTrait.description: When issuing an order to a unit, you may also attempt a second identical order to an unbroken unit within 10cm with the same roll, taking -1 if either unit has accumulated Blast markers. You may then activate them in either order (if the first unit fails its Action test it must Hold; the second may still attempt to activate later). Formations may also add +1 to any Rally test if there are friendly units within 30cm of any unit in that formation.

death-guard.name: Death Guard
death-guard.subtitle: XIV Legion · The Reapers
death-guard.lore: In the grim chronicles of the Horus Heresy, the Death Guard Legion stand apart as a force of inexorable will. The heirs of Barbarus fill their ranks with warriors who have endured challenges that would kill even their fellow Astartes, yet they emerge as unbroken blades. Led by their formidable Primarch Mortarion, they advance at a steady pace like the inevitable march of death itself, until their foes confront a force as cold and inescapable as the grave.

legionTrait.name: Indefatigable
legionTrait.description: Death Guard formations consisting of at least half Infantry units may automatically pass Action tests when taking an Advance action. In addition, their Infantry Characters are so tough they gain the Reinforced armour trait if they do not already have it.

thousand-sons.name: Thousand Sons
thousand-sons.subtitle: XV Legion · The Wisdom-Bringers
thousand-sons.lore: The Thousand Sons have always been renowned as a Legion steeped in sorcery and mysticism, as well as a force for knowledge and wisdom. Their mysterious homeworld of Prospero supplies the Legion with psykers of unparalleled potency, and they are a mirror of their enigmatic Primarch Magnus the Red. Clad in ornate, intricate armour protected as much by arcane might as cold ceramite, their glittering ranks shimmer with eldritch power as they move with otherworldly grace and preternatural precision.

legionTrait.name: Might and Magic
legionTrait.description: Thousand Sons may take 1 free Librarian upgrade per 750 points, and may upgrade any unit which has access to the Consul upgrade. In addition, units with Consul upgrades may add +1 to any Invulnerable save.

sons-of-horus.name: Sons of Horus
sons-of-horus.subtitle: XVI Legion · The Luperci
sons-of-horus.lore: History records that it is the Sons of Horus Legion who lit the spark of rebellion and stood upon the ashes of the old Imperium. They above all others stand as a byword for treason to their foes, and as exemplars of resistance to adherents. They are the force of ambition, ruthlessness, and unbowed determination.

word-bearers.name: Word Bearers
word-bearers.subtitle: XVII Legion · The Bearers of the Word
word-bearers.lore: Among the Legiones Astartes there is a group that stands alone, shrouded in malevolent darkness — the Word Bearers. Originating from the benighted world of Colchis, they are a Legion steeped in the dark arts of sorcery and religious fervour. Led by their charismatic Primarch Lorgar Aurelian, they are zealots and prophets, their every action driven by a twisted faith and a burning desire to spread the Primordial Truth. Their loyalty to their divine masters outweighs any remnant of allegiance to the Emperor.

legionTrait.name: Fanatical Zeal
legionTrait.description: Word Bearers armies may take 1 free Chaplain upgrade per 1,500 points. Additionally, when making a shooting attack or taking an Engage action against a Broken formation, add 1 additional Blast marker to the final result if any wounds were caused. You may also remove 1 additional Blast marker from any formation when making a successful Rally test.

Primarch Lorgar may only be taken once and counts as your Supreme Commander.
Ashen Circle: 1 per 2,000 pts.
Gal Vorbak Dark Brethren: 1 per 1,000 pts.
Word Bearers gain 1 free Chaplain upgrade per 1,500 points (see Legion Trait: Fanatical Zeal).

salamanders.name: Salamanders
salamanders.subtitle: XVIII Legion · Sons of Vulkan
salamanders.lore: The XVIII Legion, forged on the volcanic homeworld of Nocturne and tempered by the trials of their Primarch Vulkan. Patient, deliberate and possessed of an unyielding compassion for the innocent, the Salamanders march to war beneath drakescale cloaks, carrying flame and hammer to those who would prey upon the weak.

legionTrait.name: Promethean Cult
legionTrait.description: Salamanders units never lose a point of Firefight or Close Combat from suppression. In addition, any Salamander formation containing at least one Firedrake Terminator or Pyroclast unit may re-roll a single failed save per turn.

raven-guard.name: Raven Guard
raven-guard.subtitle: XIX Legion · The Shadowed Lords
raven-guard.lore: The XIX Legion, led by the enigmatic Primarch Corvus Corax. Masters of guerrilla warfare, infiltration and the swift killing strike, the Raven Guard slip through enemy lines on ash-black wings and depart before the foe even knows they were struck. Where lesser Legions fight battles, the Sons of Deliverance fight wars from the shadows.

legionTrait.name: Strike From the Shadows
legionTrait.description: All Raven Guard infantry formations may add +5cm to their speed on the first turn of the game. In addition, any Raven Guard formation made up entirely of units with the Scout or Infiltrate ability may be deployed using the Infiltrate special rule regardless of its formation type.

alpha-legion.name: Alpha Legion
alpha-legion.subtitle: XX Legion · The Hydra
alpha-legion.lore: Few are as at home among the shadows as the scions of the Alpha Legion. They are a shadowy and enigmatic force, embodying the virtues of secrecy, deception, and relentless pursuit of their own inscrutable agenda. Led by their Primarchs Alpharius and Omegon, they are master strategists and manipulators, weaving a web of lies and deception while operating both as clandestine kill teams and as large operational field forces.

legionTrait.name: Twin Serpents
legionTrait.description: All Character units gain the First Strike trait. Once per turn, if a Character unit is removed as a casualty and there is a friendly, unengaged, unbroken formation within 15cm, you may replace one non-character unit with a character of the same type as the one removed, so long as it may take the Consul upgrade. (Note: this may lead to formations having multiple character units.)

solar-auxilia.name: Solar Auxilia
solar-auxilia.subtitle: Excertus Imperialis · Cohorts of the Hundred Worlds
solar-auxilia.lore: The elite of the mortal Excertus Imperialis. Drawn from Terra's solar domains and moulded by the Principia Belicosa, the Solar Auxilia Cohorts march in serried ranks behind armoured spearheads of Leman Russ, Malcadors and ancient war engines. Less numerous than the Imperial Army but disciplined, professional and exquisitely equipped — the backstop of the Legiones Astartes.

specialRules.name: Legate Commanders
specialRules.description: A Solar Auxilia army may include one Legate Commander character per 750 points, or part thereof, in the army. Legate Commanders cost no points. They are added at the start of the battle before either side sets up. If the army includes a Lord Marshall, the first Legate Commander must be attached to that formation. Otherwise the first must be attached to the most expensive Line Detachment. No more than one Legate Commander per formation, and none may be attached to Imperial Navy or allied formations. Excess Legate Commanders are lost.

specialRules.name: Armoured Spearhead
specialRules.description: An army with the Armoured Spearhead special rule may garrison one formation that has more than 2 AV units per full 2,000 points, regardless of the normal restrictions for unit/formation types able to garrison. These formations may be placed on Overwatch but still count toward the maximum number of garrisoned units that may Overwatch (normally two).

imperialis-militia.name: Imperialis Militia
imperialis-militia.subtitle: Imperial Army · The Teeming Masses
imperialis-militia.lore: The numerical backbone of the Imperium. Where the Astartes break the enemy line the Imperial Army holds it — billions of soldiers drawn from a thousand worlds, led by hetmen and watched over by discipline masters. They lack the lustre of the Legions but in their hordes of machines, infantry and provincial provenances they prove that Mankind's greatest weapon is sheer mortal will.

specialRules.description: An Imperialis Militia army may include one free Discipline Master character for every full 750 points, assigned prior to the start of the battle. If the army includes a Force Commander, the first Discipline Master must be attached to the formation containing the Force Commander. Otherwise the first must be placed in the most expensive Imperialis Militia Line Detachment. Further Discipline Masters may be attached to any other Imperialis Militia formation. No more than one Discipline Master per formation, and none may be attached to an allied formation. A Rogue Psyker may be exchanged for a Discipline Master in a formation that has taken the Traitors Provenance upgrade.

specialRules.name: Provenance
specialRules.description:
Before the start of the game, select up to two of the Provenances from the list below. You may then select one of these two provenances per formation and modify the attributes of all Infantry (INF) units in the formation as indicated.
• Determined: +1 Firefight (FF) value when defending in an assault or using supporting fire.
• Feral Warriors: +1 Close Combat (CC) value.
• Survivors of the Dark Age: +1 Armour save (Armour) value.
• Traitors: gain the Berserk trait. Ogryn Brute Squad units chosen in a formation with the Traitors Provenance must choose the Chaos Spawn Mutations option. Units with the Mounted trait gain Ferocity instead of Berserk.
• Void Fighters: Grenadier units gain Jump packs and increase their movement to 30cm but may not take Standard or Heavy Transport options except the Arvus Lighter.
• Warrior Elite: +1 Firefight (FF) value when taking an Engage action.

knight-household.name: Knight Households
knight-household.subtitle: Noble Lances · Houses of Adamantium
knight-household.lore: Hereditary noble lineages whose pilots bond with towering Knight engines forged in the Martian temples. Lesser than Titans but far more responsive, the Knights of House Devine, House Vyronii and a hundred others ride to war in resplendent heraldry — and the fires of the Heresy will see those ancient names raised in glory or ground to dust.

legio-titanicus.name: Legio Titanicus
legio-titanicus.subtitle: Colleagia Titanica · The God-Machines
legio-titanicus.lore: The towering colossi of Mars — Warhounds, Reavers, Warlords and their immortal cousins. Pilots called Princeps meld their minds with the machine spirits of their Titans, walking fortresses whose tread shakes continents. The Heresy will see Legio set against Legio and the gods of battle pitted against one another.

specialRules.name: Void Shields
specialRules.description:
Some units are protected by void shield generators. Each void shield will stop one point of damage and then go down. Do not make armour saves for damage stopped by void shields, or allocate Blast markers. Once all of the shields have been knocked down, the unit may be damaged normally and you may make saving throws against any hits that are allocated and suffer Blast markers as normal.

Hits from close combat ignore void shields, but units using their firefight value must first knock down any shields before they can damage the unit. Void shields that have been knocked down can be repaired: a unit automatically repairs one downed void shield in the end phase of each turn. In addition, if a unit regroups it can use the dice roll to either repair a void shield or remove Blast markers (e.g. if you rolled a 2 you could repair 2 shields, remove 2 Blast markers, or repair 1 shield and remove 1 Blast marker).

mechanicum-taghmata.name: Mechanicum Taghmata
mechanicum-taghmata.subtitle: Cult of Mars · Phalanx of the Omnissiah
mechanicum-taghmata.lore: The techno-guard of the Mechanicum, second only to the Titan Legions in might. Adsecularis thralls, lightning-armed Thallax, insectoid Vorax and towering Thanatar cybernetica march beneath the gear-and-skull icon of Mars. As the Heresy fractures the priesthood itself, the forge-worlds align with Loyalist or Traitor, and their forbidden knowledge becomes both prize and weapon.

legio-custodes.name: Legio Custodes
legio-custodes.subtitle: The Emperor's Chosen · Companions of the Golden Throne
legio-custodes.lore: The Adeptus Custodes — golden-armoured demigods who guard the Emperor's throneroom. Each Custodian is a singular work of arcane alchemy and bonded soul-craft, their numbers small but their battle-prowess unmatched. Alongside them stand the silent Sisters of Silence, whose null-presence severs the warp from reality. As the Heresy reaches Terra, the Companions sally forth at last.

specialRules.name: The Emperor's Chosen
specialRules.description:
The Legio Custodes are famed as the Emperor's elite guard, forged by his hand by arcane alchemical means. This is represented by the following changes to the standard rules, which apply to all Legio Custodes units:
• It takes 2 Blast markers to suppress a Legio Custodes unit or kill a unit in a broken formation (ignoring any leftover Blast markers).
• Legio Custodes formations are only considered broken if they have 2 Blast markers per unit in the formation (as opposed to the standard one Blast marker per unit).
• Legio Custodes formations only count half the number of Blast markers in assault resolution, rounding down. (Note: for assault resolution, enemy formations will not receive a +1 for having no Blast markers if the Custodes formation only has 1 Blast marker before rounding down.)
• When a broken Legio Custodes formation rallies, it receives a number of Blast markers equal to the number of units, rather than half their number rounding down. Legio Custodes with the Leader trait remove 2 Blast markers instead of 1.
• Legio Custodes formations halve the number of hackdowns suffered when a formation loses an assault.
• Legio Custodes units ignore the -1 penalty to Rally tests if enemies are within 30cm.

daemons-ruinstorm.name: Daemons of the Ruinstorm
daemons-ruinstorm.subtitle: Spawn of the Warp · The Four Powers Unleashed
daemons-ruinstorm.lore: Manifest nightmares torn from the Immaterium itself. Bloodletters of Khorne, Plaguebearers of Nurgle, Daemonettes of Slaanesh and the capricious Horrors of Tzeentch march alongside renegades who made bargains too dark to name. They are unpredictable, uncontrollable, and infinitely hungry — the Warmaster harnesses their malice at terrible cost, for the gods of Chaos demand payment in souls.

legionTrait.name: Echoes of the Ruinstorm
legionTrait.description: Strategy Rating 2 · Initiative 2+. 1 Support per Line, 0-1 Lord of War per Line, any number of Upgrades per formation (each only once). Special Rules: Instability (failed Action test costs 1D3 Lesser Daemons), Chaos Gate, Daemonic Assault.

compositionRules: Strategy Rating 2, Initiative 2+.
1 Support detachment per Line; 0-1 Lord of War per Line (same god affiliation).
Any number of Upgrades per formation, each only once.
Special: Instability — Line detachment failing an Action test loses 1D3 Lesser Daemons (no Blast markers).
Special: Chaos Gate — keep up to 3 formations in the Warp; deploy via Chaos Gate objective.
Special: Daemonic Assault — 1 formation per 1,000 pts may deploy by Teleport from turn 2 (max 1 Greater Daemon per teleporting formation).
Optional Trait: Purposeful — single-god/Undivided force gains +2 Strategic Rating and 1 reroll/turn.

specialRules.name: Instability
specialRules.description: Any Line detachment that fails an Action test, for any reason, immediately loses 1D3 Lesser Daemons (INF). These losses do not cause any additional Blast markers.

specialRules.name: Chaos Gate
specialRules.description: Chaos Gates are breaches in the walls of reality that allow the forces of Chaos direct access from their foul realms. A Chaos Gate included in the army allows the Chaos player to pick up to three other formations and keep them within the Warp instead of deploying them normally. Any formations kept within the Warp may enter play via the Chaos Gate by taking an action that allows them to make a move, then measuring their first move from the centre of the Chaos Gate objective marker. No more than one formation may deploy from a Chaos Gate each turn.

specialRules.name: Daemonic Assault
specialRules.description: Before deployment, the Daemon player may nominate one formation in their army per 1,000 points of this Army List to deploy by Teleport. These formations may only enter play from the second turn on, and may contain any units, with the restriction that the formation may only include a single Greater Daemon unit.
